purpose: Plain-language model used by GPTOPT Guided Mode to translate technical audit values, player setup, and game profile checks into user decisions.

productDirection.scope: Competitive PC gaming optimization platform, not a Halo-only script pack.
productDirection.referenceProfile: Halo Infinite is the first proving-ground profile because it is the user's best live test case.
futureProfiles: Marvel Rivals or other current shooters
productDirection.rule: Separate universal PC health checks from game-specific profiles, controller profiles, warmup routines, and player-review guidance.

Good.meaning: This item is already in the expected state.

Review.meaning: This may be fine depending on the selected game, hardware, or player preference.
Review.userAction: Read the explanation before changing anything.

Fix.meaning: This is likely wrong for the selected profile.
Fix.userAction: Use the recommended fix, then audit again.

Learn.meaning: This setting or habit should be tested, tracked, and improved over time.
Learn.userAction: Run a focused test, record the result, and let GPTOPT update the recommendation.

principles: Default to plain-language readiness, not raw registry names.
Every fix must explain what it changes and whether it is reversible.
Hide technical detail until the user asks for it.
Beginner mode should answer: ready to play, fix first, or review.
Advanced mode may show raw values, paths, registry keys, and process names.
Never require the user to understand HwSchMode, OverlayTestMode, SyncLimiter, or timer APIs before making a safe decision.
Do not assume Halo is the whole product. Halo is a test profile for the broader competitive PC gaming engine.
Every game profile should define display mode, limiter source, input path, audio path, overlays, telemetry tools, and warmup routine.
Player habits matter. GPTOPT should learn from controller feel, warmup results, match notes, and before/after performance reports.

layers.universalPc: Windows, GPU, timer, power, USB, network, audio, overlays, background load, rollback safety.
layers.gameProfile: Per-game executable, config files, FPS cap, display mode, graphics preset, launch stack, anti-cheat constraints.
layers.inputProfile: Controller or mouse settings, vendor software, deadzones, curves, sensitivity, acceleration, virtual input conflicts.
layers.playerRoutine: Warmup, aim drills, ranked readiness, VOD review notes, session fatigue, confidence tracking.
layers.learningLoop: Compare audit state, player feel, and match results to improve future recommendations.

universalChecks.goodWhen: Current timer resolution is 1 ms or lower, preferably 0.5 ms during competitive sessions.
universalChecks.plainFix: The timer is not in low-latency mode, which can make input and frame pacing less consistent.
universalChecks.risk: Low. A background helper process holds the timer request. Stop it or disable the scheduled task to undo.

universalChecks.goodWhen: HAGS=2, MPO OverlayTestMode=5, Game Mode enabled, capture disabled, no stale DWM tweak leftovers.
universalChecks.plainGood: Windows graphics and capture settings are in the expected gaming state.
universalChecks.plainFix: A Windows graphics/capture setting is outside the selected baseline.
universalChecks.risk: Medium-low. Registry changes are reversible and should be backed up before writing.

universalChecks.goodWhen: Required controller app/service is running and the intended controller device is present.
universalChecks.plainFix: Controller software or service is not running.
universalChecks.recommendedAction: Start Session Apps or open the controller app.
universalChecks.risk: Low. Starts existing vendor software only.

universalChecks.goodWhen: Expected audio device exists; required routing software is running when needed.
universalChecks.plainReview: The audio device exists but the routing app may not be running. This can be fine unless headset routing sounds wrong.
universalChecks.recommendedAction: Open the headset/audio app if audio routing is wrong.
universalChecks.risk: Low. Do not disable required audio software.

gameProfiles.name: Halo Infinite
gameProfiles.role: Reference profile and live testing target.
gameProfiles.status: Stable/maintenance-era game profile; recommendations should not chase constant seasonal changes unless a new patch changes behavior.

checks.goodWhen: HaloInfinite.exe.cfg has FramerateLimit=240 and ApplicationDetectionLevel is not 0.
checks.plainGood: RTSS is set as the Halo FPS limiter at 240 FPS.
checks.plainFix: RTSS is installed, but Halo is not clearly capped by its own RTSS profile.
checks.risk: Low. GPTOPT backs up the RTSS profile before changing it.

checks.goodWhen: 3840x2160, render scale 100, UI scale 100, VSync off, min/target 960 for the user's current baseline.
checks.plainGood: Halo display settings match the current competitive baseline.
checks.plainFix: Halo display settings do not match the selected baseline.
checks.recommendedAction: Apply Halo Display Baseline after backup.
checks.risk: Low. GPTOPT backs up SpecControlSettings.json first and only changes listed display/session fields.

controller.currentKnownUserBaseline: Flydigi Vader 4 Pro wired, SpaceStation required, no smoothing, no look acceleration, curves tested at 0/0 and 3/3.

warmup.goal: Prepare hands, aim, movement, and decision speed before ranked sessions.
defaultRoutine: 5 minutes BR/bandit precision and reticle discipline
5 minutes map movement or curb-slide routes
1 low-stress match or bot/custom drill before ranked

proStudy.purpose: Learn patterns from elite players without pretending their exact settings automatically fit every user.
track: Ninja historical Halo habits and communication style
when pros disengage instead of ego-challenging
proStudy.rule: Use pro study to generate drills and decision rules, not blindly copy sensitivity/settings without testing.

nextProductCapabilities: Game profile selector instead of Halo-only buttons.
Controller profile section with plain-language feel goals: steady, snappy, micro-aim, tracking, anti-overaim.
Warmup mode that launches a checklist before ranked sessions.
Session review mode that asks only a few focused questions after play and updates recommendations.
Knowledge refresh workflow that researches current patches, pro meta, driver notes, and tool changes before changing recommendations.
Recommended action queue that explains impact, risk, backup, and undo before every fix.
